description: Coulson and Ward travel together to Ohio to investigate Brian Hayward's sister at a university. Their journey features personal conversation, with Coulson reflecting on a past romantic relationship with a cellist, regretfully recounting his experiences after being declared dead post-Avengers. Ward listens, showing a rare moment of understanding between the two agents. This scene provides insight into Coulson's character and past sacrifices.

transcript.line: “Psychology of women: Unraveling the female psyche.”
It's a whole course. Hayward's sister's in there now.
We'll be at the school in 10. We'll catch her on her way out.
Time was, you just had to figure them out. Solving the puzzle was half the fun.
You solve a lot of puzzles in your day?
A few. Some more worth it than others. One was... especially rewarding.
Oh. I guess she wasn't level 7, sir?

She was a cellist, second chair, Portland symphony. Saw her play whenever I was in town. Ever see a beautiful woman play the cello? It's something else. She laughed at my jokes, too, which was a very nice bonus.
You know where she is now?
I was gone for months. The Avengers thought I was dead, so I had to keep that up... S.H.I.E.L.D. protocol. It's probably better this way.
Maybe if she'd been part of the agency...
In my experience, that's just asking for trouble.
